steps.text: Work with the directives officer to archive expired and canceled directives.
Provide the Director, Directorate of Administrative Programs, a copy of the procedure and any future amendments.
Submit NPIs to the web administrator for publication.
Routinely review published NPIs to ensure information accurately reflects the Agency's current policies and procedures.
Update or archive outdated NPIs as appropriate.
Informally circulate drafts, reports, and other materials relating to a directive drafting.
Consult with DEP and DCSP for federal program change language during the informal clearance.
Ensure construction-related directives are reviewed by DOC.
Review the draft directive. If necessary, request an extension from the originator as soon as possible.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator. Alternatively, sends concurrence or comments electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.).
Negotiate the acceptance of comments with the originator or originating Program Director or presents the case to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate for approval clearance.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and requests the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Coordinate union review through the Labor Management Relations Officer.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the union, delayed posting request, or pending press release.
Provide the final 508-complaint PDF file directive to the web administrator for posting to the Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage within the requested timeframe.
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Convene a meeting with directorates and regional offices to provide information on the content of the direction and provide an opportunity for discussion of initial questions and issues.
Provide a copy of the proposed direction to each clearance office required to provide clearance.
Consider the proposed direction and provide comments.
Elevate outstanding issue(s) to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary, and as necessary, to the Assistant Secretary, for a decision regarding the issue of contention.
Provide each directive with a classification code and numeric number based on the type of directive.
Assign consecutive numbers (directives officer for national directives, Regional Administrators for regional directives).
Classify the OSHA Instruction according to primary, secondary, and tertiary classification codes and sequentially number according to the primary classification.

Number the OSHA Notice using the calendar year and a two-digit sequential number followed by the subject's primary and secondary codes (or if for cancellation, use the last two digits of the calendar year and a two-digit sequential number followed by the OSHA instruction number).
Number the OSHA Direction the same as Notices, except precede the numbering sequence with the DIR identifier.

Number Regional Instructions using the primary classification code, the secondary classification, and the tertiary classification (the regional number followed by the year and consecutive number for the year).
Assign the same number to Supplemental Regional Directives as the corresponding national instruction.
Number Regional Notices using the last two-digit calendar year, a two-digit regional number, and the two-digit sequential number followed by the subject's primary and secondary codes.
Retain the original sequential number for revisions of directives, updating the effective date to indicate the most recent version.
Include only the most recent issuance on the directives list on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Review non-substantive changes by the Program Director (or Regional Administrator) of the initiating office, the directives officer, and the Office of the Solicitor.

Review and approve non-significant changes by the Program Director (or Regional Administrator) of the initiating office, additional directorates with expertise, the Directorate of Administrative Programs, the directives officer, the Assistant Secretary’s Office, and the Office of the Solicitor (or appropriate Regional Office of the Solicitor).
Submit significant changes to undergo the normal clearance process as described in Chapter 3.
Implement and comply with the provisions of this instruction and with related instructions governing the management of policy and non-policy issues.
Self-assess and routinely monitor the directive process using the checklist on the directives tools webpage to ensure compliance with the provisions of the instruction.
Review and assess active directives on an annual basis.
Maintain a final document of the review findings in the national office or regional files.
Delegate the authority of review and assessment, as well as responsibility for preparing a final document of the review findings, as appropriate.
Conduct a full assessment of the files for the directives approved that year and ensure that all appropriate documents are included in the file.
Send an email or memo to the Director, Office of Audit, DAP, or designee documenting the completion of the requirement.
Provide documentation of completion to the directives officer.
Retain completion documentation in the directive file record.
Confirm the self-assessment documentation was received within the established deadline.
Include the auditing of the assessment process as part of audit responsibilities and ensure that the directives process is part of regular auditing tasks, as appropriate.
National and regional offices submit webpage updates to the directives officer.
Review the OSHA Directives webpage biennially for accuracy in coordination with national offices, regional offices, and the web administrator.
Archive directives containing an expiration date automatically.
Provide notification to national office directors and regional administrators of expiring directives for the upcoming fiscal year in Q4 of the current fiscal year.
Archive directives that are canceled by reference in the cancellation paragraph of a new or revised instruction, notice, or direction upon approval of the request.
Keep the OSHA Notice for the purpose of directive cancellation on the active publications page for 90 days.
Archive the cancellation notice at the end of the 90 days.
Apply the normal clearance process as described in this instruction for significant changes.
Refer to Appendix A for national clearance process.

preconditions.text: Ensure all NPIs are 508 compliant before submission for publication.
Before a direction may be drafted
Before a direction may go into effect
If a resolution is unable to be reached
The directive is an OSHA Instruction
The directive is an OSHA Notice
The directive is an OSHA Direction

If a regional notice is issued for cancellation of an instruction, number it using the last two digits of the calendar year, a two-digit regional number, and the two-digit sequential number followed by the regional instruction number.
The changes are non-substantive (correcting typographical errors, grammar, syntax, minor factual inaccuracies, or updating outdated references).
The changes are non-significant (do not significantly alter the requirements of the directive).
The changes are significant (major substantive changes that change the purpose or intent, or substantially alter OSHA policies and procedures).
The changes are significant (major substantive changes, such as those that change the purpose or intent of the directive, or substantially alter OSHA policies and procedures).

constraints.text: Clearance offices have up to five working days from the date of the OSHA 201 to consider the proposed direction and provide comments.
Revisions of directives will not receive new numbers.
Only the most recent issuance will be included on the directives list on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Full clearance as described in Chapter 3 is not required for non-significant changes.
For extensive directives posted as web pages (FOM, SHMS, OTM) that are updated by individual chapters, only the chapter being updated must be sent through the clearance process.
No later than September 30 of the fiscal year
In the first quarter of each fiscal year
Requests submitted directly to the web administrator will be returned to be submitted by the directives officer.

All archived documents must include the following banner: 'NOTICE: This is an OSHA Archive Document and may no longer represent OSHA Policy. It is presented here as historical content, for research and review purposes only. Return to current directives.'
